on word completion with momentum preserved.

commit.message:
fix(kiln): flat-on-top only for rectangular tiles with face-only glaze

Flat-on-top fraction (flat_on_edge_coefficient) was computed for all
shapes/glazes that survived the early-exit checks. Two cases were wrong:

- triangle shape: triangles fire back-to-back as pairs on edge; placing
  them flat on top of standing rows is physically unsafe
- face-2-edges glaze: tile's glazed edges touch adjacent standing tiles
  → glaze damage; flat-on-top is blocked for this glaze type

Guard added: flat_on_top_allowed = (shape == "rectangle" and glaze == "face-only")
When guard is False, flat_per_level = 0 so total is edge-only.

commit.message:
feat: persistent ROM index connects boot scan to smart-join

Boot scan now writes rom_index.json with every confirmed ROM (path,
system, core). rom_finder loads this index as source 0 before any
filesystem walk, so arcade ROMs discovered at boot are found instantly
at join time — no re-scan, no fuzzy guessing, no IA download loop.

Also fixes: MAME subdirectory ROMs (mame/roms/*.zip) now found by
checking both flat and one-level-deep paths during boot scan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: kill agent process group in force_kill() to prevent orphans (#142)

* fix: kill agent process group in force_kill() to prevent orphans

When force_kill() sends SIGKILL to the shepherd, its cleanup code never
runs, so it never forwards SIGTERM to the child. The agent becomes an
orphan reparented to PID 1 and keeps running indefinitely.

The shepherd now writes the child PID to a .child-pid file after spawn,
and force_kill() kills the child's process group before killing the
shepherd. All cleanup/reconnect paths in app.rs also handle the new file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add coverage for force_kill and child PID cleanup

- force_kill kills child process group (spawns real processes, verifies signal delivery)
- force_kill without child_pid only kills shepherd
- kill_child_from_pid_file kills process group from file
- kill_child_from_pid_file with missing file is a no-op
- remove_session cleans up .child-pid file
- child_pid_path derives correct extension

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): multi-provider support + skills-update reliability

Ollama/multi-provider fixes:
- openclaw.json.j2: conditional contextWindow (200k/128k/32k) and input
  modalities (text+image vs text-only) per provider (anthropic/openai/other)
- litellm_callback.py: replace Claude-priced _DEFAULT_RATES with _ZERO_RATES
  so unrecognised models (Ollama, etc.) log cost_usd=0 instead of wrong cost
- deploy.sh: default Ollama URL → host.docker.internal:11434 (works in Docker)

Skills-update reliability fixes:
- macos-native.yml: add explicit git fetch + reset --hard after Ansible git
  module (force:yes only does checkout --force against local ref, not remote
  tracking branch; this caused stale skills-registry.yaml on Mac Mini)
- Makefile SKILLS_UPDATE_PY: check subdir exists before rename, catch broad
  Exception alongside CalledProcessError so a missing subdir path prints a
  diagnostic and continues rather than crashing make skills-update entirely

Schema loading fix:
- docker-compose.yml.j2: remove deleted hardcoded namespace schema paths
  (scilit.tql, jobhunt.tql, apm.tql, techrecon.tql); replace with auto-
  discovery via find /local_skills -name schema.tql (mirrors make build-db)

commit.message:
feat(m6): RBAC-aware UI controls — role-based button disabling + auth context (#103)

* feat(m6): RBAC-aware UI controls — role-based button disabling + auth context (Phase 4)

Add optimistic client-side RBAC enforcement matching Agent-5's 4-level
role hierarchy (viewer < analyst < experimenter < admin). Backend still
enforces security; UI disables buttons and shows tooltips for clarity.

New files:
- auth.ts: UserRole types, hasAtLeast(), UI_PERMISSIONS map
- auth-context.tsx: AuthProvider + useAuth() hook with dev role switcher
- auth-rbac.test.tsx: 18 tests covering auth utilities, context, and page RBAC

Modified:
- api.ts: X-User-Email/X-User-Role header injection, RpcError class, isPermissionDenied()
- layout.tsx: AuthProvider wraps all children
- nav-header.tsx: Role badge, user email, dev-only role switcher dropdown
- state-actions.tsx: Buttons disabled when role insufficient with tooltip
- page.tsx: "New Experiment" disabled for viewer/analyst
- experiments/new: "Insufficient Permissions" banner for unauthorized roles
- experiments/[id]: VariantForm gated on experimenter+, permission error handling
- handlers.ts: Mock auth enforcement with enableMockAuth()/checkAuth()
- Existing tests wrapped with AuthProvider (218 tests pass)

commit.message:
feat: plan/build separation, validator agent, PostToolUse hook

Addresses feedback from ralph loop design review:

Plan/build mode distinction:
- Add commands/plan.md: planning-only loop that generates IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
  as a prioritised checkbox task list; verification suggests test -f plan file
- Add agents/planner.md: Opus-tier, read-only strategic agent for gap analysis,
  priority reasoning, and plan generation — separates strategic reasoning from
  Sonnet-tier design work in architect
- solo.md: check for IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md and work from it when present
- team.md: reference plan file as initial task queue; add planner to persona list
- CLAUDE.md: add plan command to commands table

Validator agent:
- Add agents/validator.md: Read/Bash/Grep only, single-instance constraint,
  reports PASS/FAIL mechanically; never modifies code
- Separates test-writing (tester) from test-running (validator) per the feedback
  that the tester should not render judgment on its own tests
- team.md: validator in all persona templates

Model routing fix:
- team.md: named personas use their agent frontmatter model (planner=opus,
  others=sonnet); --speed is default-only for unnamed agents

PostToolUse validation hook:
- Add hooks/post-tool-validate.sh: fires after Edit/Write on source files,
  sources .ralpha-validate.conf from project root (opt-in), runs TYPECHECK_CMD
  and/or LINT_CMD, exits non-zero to block the agent on failure
- hooks.json: register PostToolUse on Edit|Write, 30s timeout

README fixes:
- Clarify promise UX: Claude must output <promise>PHRASE</promise> tags, not
  just the phrase; quick-start examples now show the full workflow
- Add consumer project setup section: minimal CLAUDE.md template (build/test/
  lint commands) + .ralpha-validate.conf pattern for per-edit validation
- Update components table: 6 commands, 7 agents, 6 hooks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(m5): surrogate recalibration trigger via Kafka (#102)

Replace the TODO in TriggerSurrogateRecalibration with a Kafka publish to
the `surrogate_recalibration_requests` topic, enabling M3 to begin
recalibration asynchronously without querying M5 back.

- New `surrogate` package: Publisher interface, KafkaPublisher (hash
  balancer, keyed by model_id, RequireOne acks), MemPublisher for tests
- RecalibrationRequest envelope with all fields M3 needs
- WithSurrogatePublisher ServiceOption for dependency injection
- Handler publishes best-effort (log warning on failure, don't fail RPC)
- Wired in main.go alongside existing Kafka broker config
- Kafka topic: 4 partitions, 30-day retention, RF=3, ISR=2
- Integration test with MemPublisher verifying all published fields
- 3 unit tests: publish/retrieve, reset, JSON snake_case roundtrip

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: detect and block SVG ad containers (Google Ads)

Ad networks like Google Ads render images inside SVG elements using
<image href="..."> children. Previously all SVGs were skipped as icons.
Now SVGs containing <image> elements are treated as regular images:
discovered, classified, and blockable via right-click context menu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Suppress react-hooks/set-state-in-effect for intentional hydration-bridging setState calls in useOfflineStatus

commit.message:
fix: race condition in client vista — add sesionUid to deps

When sesionUid loads from RTDB after the effect already ran,
tieneConexionVigente() returned false even if the user had paid.
Now the effect re-evaluates when sesionUid arrives, and also
handles transitions from conexion/alias states when data loads late.
